goal: Multi-platform support research and PlatformAdapter interface extraction for gruAI

risks: 1. Premature abstraction: Extracting an interface from only one implementation (Claude Code) risks designing the wrong abstractions. Mitigation: the research deliverable catalogs 3-4 platform data formats to validate the interface shape before coding.
2. session-state.ts is the hardest file (830+ lines, tight coupling to JSONL format). Refactoring it behind an interface while maintaining zero behavior change requires careful test coverage.
3. Scope creep into foreman.ts and pipeline spawn logic. The adapter should cover session parsing + file watching + config discovery only. Foreman and pipeline adapters are Wave 2.
over_engineering_flags: Creating adapter interfaces for ALL coupling points when only session parsing and file watching are the core data-flow concern.
Building a plugin registry or dynamic loader when a simple static import per platform is sufficient for v1.
Over-specifying the research report with competitive matrices and market sizing when the real deliverable is: what data does each platform emit, and what interface shape covers them?
challenges.recommendation: Simplify. Scope the PlatformAdapter to session parsing + file watching + config discovery only. Foreman spawn adapter and pipeline adapter are Wave 2 concerns. Research should focus on data format comparison, not market analysis (brainstorm already did market sizing).

projects.title: Multi-Platform Research Report + PlatformAdapter Interface Extraction
projects.scope_summary: Three sequential deliverables in one project. First: research report documenting session data formats, file layouts, and spawn patterns for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Aider, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline — focused on what each platform emits and where it stores state. Second: architectural plan defining the PlatformAdapter interface shape (session parsing, file watching, config discovery) validated against the research findings. Third: extract the Claude Code-specific code in session-state.ts, session-scanner.ts, session-watcher.ts, claude-watcher.ts, and config.ts behind the PlatformAdapter interface — pure refactor, zero behavior change, all existing tests must pass identically.
